name: 尚穆根
current_positions: Member of the Parliament of Singapore

work_experience.position: Senior Partner; Head of Litigation and Dispute Resolution
Member of Parliament (Sembawang GRC 1988-2011, Nee Soon GRC since 2011)

person_relationships.description: Both investigated in 2023 Ridout Road rental matter; CPIB found no wrongdoing. Jointly sued Lee Hsien Yang for defamation over Ridout Road statements.
Tong succeeded Shanmugam as Minister for Law in 2025. Both were partners at Allen & Gledhill.
Served in Lee's cabinet from 2008; represented Lee and other PMs in civil libel lawsuits as a lawyer
Sued Lee Hsien Yang for defamation in 2023 over Ridout Road rental statements; High Court ruled in Shanmugam's favour

political_stances.stance_content: Reaffirmed Singapore's stance against illegal protests, citing the need to safeguard national stability and prevent foreign interference. External actors exploit unrest to destabilise nations.
political_stances.source: Asia Future Summit / The Online Citizen

political_stances.stance_content: Warning that identity politics is a one-way street to ruin. Must continue to take an unequivocal stance to reject the mixing of race, religion and politics.
Defended Singapore's anti-drug stance and the presumptions under the Misuse of Drugs Act, stating the government's position is based on evidence.
Foreign interference in Singapore's politics and policies is an absolute no, including penalties for criminal offences imposed by foreign entities.
Led the Select Committee on Deliberate Online Falsehoods; questioned Facebook on Cambridge Analytica data misuse. Resulted in the Protection from Online Falsehoods and Manipulation Act (POFMA).
Led changes to criminal breach of trust laws and Penal Code review including greater protections against sexual offences.

major_achievements.achievement: One of the youngest lawyers appointed Senior Counsel at age 38
Known as one of the 'twin titans of litigation' alongside Davinder Singh; over 100 cases reported in law reports
Served as President of the Singapore Indian Development Association
Steered the passage of the Foreign Interference (Countermeasures) Act (FICA)
Launched book 'Policy, Fairness and Compassion' collecting his speeches over 38 years in politics

biography_summary: K. Shanmugam is a Singaporean politician and lawyer who has served as Coordinating Minister for National Security since 2025 and Minister for Home Affairs since 2015. A Tamil Indian, he was one of the youngest lawyers to be appointed Senior Counsel at age 38 in 1998. Known as a 'titan of litigation', he practised at Drew & Napier and Allen & Gledhill, representing PMs Lee Kuan Yew, Goh Chok Tong, and Lee Hsien Loong in lawsuits. He entered politics in 1988, serving as MP for Sembawang GRC and later Nee Soon GRC. He has been Minister for Law (2008-2025), Foreign Affairs (2011-2015), and Home Affairs (2010-2011, 2015-present). He was investigated in the 2023 Ridout Road rental matter and cleared. He is married to clinical psychologist Seetha Subbiah (previously to Jothie Rajah) and has two children.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with family members, political stances on law enforcement/race/foreign interference, major achievements, person relationships from Wikipedia and web search
